title: Narrow harness change without unrelated refactors
prompt: Add a small status summary improvement to Harness CLI that shows completed and blocked feature counts when metrics are available. Do not refactor provider adapters or change the harness run loop.
judgeFocus: whether the evaluator penalized unrelated churn

scale.1: Fails the dimension or has no usable evidence.
scale.2: Has major gaps, regressions, or mostly claim-based evidence.
scale.3: Acceptable but incomplete, noisy, or only partially evidenced.
scale.4: Strong result with minor gaps or limited missing evidence.
scale.5: Excellent result with complete behavior and strong evidence.

criticalRequirements: Status output must show completed and blocked feature counts when metrics are available.
Provider adapters and the harness run loop must not be refactored or behaviorally changed.
Existing status behavior must remain usable when metrics are missing.
The diff must stay narrow and proportional to the requested summary improvement.

dimensions.description: How completely the final workspace adds the requested completed/blocked status summary without changing the requested behavior.
Whether build/tests pass and status output handles available, missing, zero, completed, and blocked metrics correctly.
Scope discipline, minimality, maintainability, avoidance of provider/run-loop churn, and fit with existing CLI formatting.
Quality of research, planning, contract specificity, handoffs, and whether scope limits were preserved through implementation and evaluation.
Whether verdicts explicitly checked for unrelated churn and grounded scope claims in diffs, commands, and artifacts.

scoringNotes: Do not change this rubric based on run-generated eval criteria or contracts.
Any provider adapter or run-loop modification should strongly penalize implementationQuality unless the run proves it was required.
A tiny correct change with clear validation should beat a larger featureful change.
